source_intake_record.raw_question_text: On a frictionless horizontal surface, two bodies make a head-on collision and stick together. Body 1 has a mass of 3.50 kg and initially moves to the right with speed $7.0 \frac{\mathrm{~m}}{\mathrm{~s}}$. Body 2 initially is at rest. What is the mass of Body 2?

image_dependency: question_text does not state the post-collision velocity, so the numeric mass in answer_text is not derivable from text alone
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows 1 associated image exists and registry_passed=true
the mismatch between textual givens and the specific numeric answer suggests missing visual information is likely carried by the image
multi_step: solving for Body 2 mass requires combining multiple physics conditions: perfectly inelastic collision plus conservation of momentum
the task likely also requires extracting an additional value from the associated image before doing the algebra
answer_type is short_text with a precise numeric result, consistent with a multi-step calculation rather than direct lookup
verifiability: answer_text is a single numeric value, which is straightforward to check against a physics calculation
registry_passed=true and answer_present=true support basic downstream validation feasibility
verifiability is reduced because the needed input appears incomplete in question_text and may require image-based extraction first
